name: pdd
description: PDD (Parity-Driven Development) — framework for reliable legacy refactor/rewrite/port with tracked behavioral parity.
skillInstructions: PDD is command-based: the user invokes /audit-bootstrap, /audit-new, /audit-investigate, /audit-resolve, /audit-compare, /audit-qa <env>, /audit-pr, /audit-status (Kimi Code also matches skills by description). When a Superpowers-style instruction says to ask the user, use Kimi Code's AskUserQuestion. When it refers to a subagent, use Kimi Code's Agent tool. When beginning PDD work, run `pdd check` in the terminal and, if it reports an update, offer `pdd update`. Full method and reference: AGENTS.md.

interface.shortDescription: Refactor, rewrite, and port legacy systems with tracked behavioral parity.
interface.longDescription: PDD turns "does the new system still behave like the old one?" into objective, tracked evidence — a finding at a time, gated through QA before it reaches main.
